title: The neighbour's invitation
setting: Your neighbour, Mrs. Bucher, greets you in the hallway and invites you over.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Grüezi! Sie sind dr Nöi, gäll?
steps.en: Hello! You're the new one, right?

options.es: Grüezi! Ja, ich bi grad iizoge.
options.en: Hello! Yes, I just moved in.
options.feedback: Warm and clear. Returning 'Grüezi' to an elder neighbour is just right.
A little short for a friendly neighbour. A few words make a better first impression.
Far too guarded! She's welcoming you. Meet her warmth.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Willkomme! Ich bi d'Frau Bucher, vo näbedra.
steps.en: Welcome! I'm Mrs. Bucher, from next door.

options.en: Pleased to meet you! I'm Tom.
options.feedback: 'Fröit mi' plus your name — a polished, friendly introduction.
Friendly, but 'Hoi' is quite casual for a first meeting with an older neighbour. 'Grüezi' or 'fröit mi' fits better.
Gives your name, but 'fröit mi, ich heisse Tom' is warmer for a first meeting.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Wänd Sie spöter uf en Kafi verbiicho?
steps.en: Would you like to come by for a coffee later?

options.feedback: Enthusiastic and gracious — exactly how to accept a kind invitation.
Simple and warm. A perfectly good yes.
Turning down a neighbour's first warm gesture stings. If you can, accept — this is how you build a life here.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Schön! Denn bis spöter. Am Sächsi?
steps.en: Lovely! Then see you later. At six?

options.es: Am Sächsi passt mer. Bis denn!
options.en: Six works for me. See you then!
options.feedback: Confirming the time and a warm 'bis denn' — perfect close.
Warm and natural. You've made a good first impression.
Non-committal after she set a time feels a little cold. If you mean yes, say yes warmly.
